claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements a boundary audit system at src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts:97-121, 566-597 to detect project file mutations during agent execution, and converts any detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers at lines 585-597.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA-256 hashes of all project files except allowed_write_roots, and diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after states to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit mechanism that detects file mutations

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() captures boundaryBefore snapshot before agent execution loop and boundaryAfter snapshot after all agents complete.
evidence.why: Shows boundary audit integration in execution flow

evidence.summary: Boundary diff is computed and stored in result.boundary with project_mutation flag set to 'violated' or 'clean'.
Boundary mutations are converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and added to readiness.blockers array, forcing prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Mutation-to-blocker conversion that gates PRD readiness

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary sets project_mutation='forbidden' and allowed_write_roots=[output_dir] to constrain agent write permissions.
Agent prompt explicitly instructs 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and declares allowed artifact root.
evidence.why: Agent instructions that set boundary expectations

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false for all protocols.
requiredEvidenceRoles() returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] for cross_check policy and ['explorer', 'verifier'] for single_agent policy.
evidence.why: Evidence role requirements based on policy

evidence.summary: inspectDemandTriage() sets evidence_policy='cross_check' when technicalRisk or highCost signals detected, and route='careful' for hard triggers.
evidence.why: Triage logic that selects evidence policy

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() validates that evidence with project scope includes repo-relative path locators.
evidence.why: Project evidence validation that prevents unverified facts

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() executes Claude CLI with configured tools and returns structured result including stdout, stderr, exit code.
evidence.why: Agent execution mechanism that boundary audit wraps

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs Claude CLI arguments with --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools for tool profile control.
evidence.why: Tool profile configuration that affects agent capabilities

assumptions: The boundary audit system has not been stress-tested with full-tool Claude agents in a dirty working tree to verify detection accuracy.
The current working tree status may affect boundary baseline detection.
SHA-256 file digest computation at evidence-dispatch.ts:87-95 may have edge cases with symlinks, special files, or concurrent modifications.
risks: If boundary audit fails to detect mutations (false negative), PRD readiness could be granted despite project corruption.
If boundary audit produces false positives, valid evidence gathering workflows could be incorrectly blocked.
If agents execute before boundary snapshot is captured, mutations would not be detected.
If allowed_write_roots configuration is incorrect, legitimate agent writes could be flagged as violations or real violations could be missed.
missing: Integration test or manual verification that boundary audit detects mutations when full-tool Claude agents are executed in evidence dispatch.
Evidence that boundary audit correctly handles dirty working tree state where uncommitted changes exist before agent execution.
Verification that allowed_write_roots exclusion at evidence-dispatch.ts:104 correctly permits writes to .yolo/demand/evidence/* but not to other project locations.
Test coverage or evidence showing boundary behavior when agents attempt to modify files outside allowed roots.

result.notes: Explorer confirms boundary audit implementation exists at specified paths with mutation-to-blocker conversion. Independent verification recommended for stress-test scenarios and dirty working tree behavior.
